Table 1: Methodological positioning of AD-RaNN among representative randomized neural network solvers. Here “low” / “high” refer to the dimension or cost of hidden-parameter adaptation relative to the hidden-layer size. The methods are ordered roughly from fully fixed hidden representations to increasingly adaptive hidden-parameter strategies.

Table 4: Comparison of PINN, SNN, Fre-based, differential-evolution-based, and PDAD methods for the Poisson problem (3.3). For the Fre-based, differential-evolution-based, and PDAD methods, we report the hidden-layer neurons (HLN), distribution parameters p = (rx, ry), runtime (s), and relative ℓ2 error; the ridge parameter λ is reported only for PDAD. For PINN and SNN, the HLN column reports the network architecture, together with the corresponding runtime and relative ℓ2 error.
